steps.title: Focus on musical traditions
steps.content: The underlined claim is not just that nueva cancion spread geographically. It says that as the movement spread beyond Chile, the range of musical traditions built into its foundation also expanded. The best detail must therefore name a non-Chilean musical tradition that influenced songs associated with the movement.

steps.title: Separate theme from tradition
steps.content: Choice A discusses political commentary from shared colonial histories, but political subject matter is not itself a musical tradition. Choice C names the quena, an Andean flute used in Chile as well as other countries, so it does not clearly show expansion beyond the original Chilean foundation. Choice D describes where artists were from, not what musical forms were incorporated.

steps.title: Choose the outside form
steps.content: Choice B identifies corrido, a genre of narrative songs from Mexico, as a stylistic influence on many nueva cancion songs. Because Mexico is outside Chile and corrido is specifically a musical genre, this detail directly illustrates the movement incorporating a broader set of musical traditions as it spread. The answer is B.
